tools.name: vibekit_list_apps
tools.description: List all hosted apps in your VibeKit account, each with its id, subdomain, status, and live URL. Start here to get an appId for the other app tools.

tools.name: vibekit_get_app
tools.description: Get full details for one hosted app — status, subdomain/URL, repo, runtime, and database/domain config. Use the appId from vibekit_list_apps.

tools.name: vibekit_create_app
tools.description: Create a new hosted app from a starter template and deploy it live at <subdomain>.vibekit.bot. Call vibekit_list_templates first to pick a valid template. To deploy existing code from a GitHub repo instead, use vibekit_deploy.

tools.name: vibekit_list_templates
tools.description: List the starter templates available to vibekit_create_app (e.g. landing, dashboard, blog, saas, crud-api). Call this before vibekit_create_app to choose a valid template.

tools.name: vibekit_deploy
tools.description: Deploy a GitHub repo to VibeKit hosting, live at <subdomain>.vibekit.bot (Fargate container behind nginx, SSL automatic). Use when the code already lives in a GitHub repo; for a starter template instead use vibekit_create_app.

tools.name: vibekit_redeploy
tools.description: Redeploy an existing app to pull and ship the latest code. Use after pushing changes to the app's GitHub repo.

tools.name: vibekit_app_logs
tools.description: Fetch recent container/application logs for an app. Use this to debug crashes, errors, or a deploy that isn't serving.

tools.name: vibekit_restart_app
tools.description: Restart a hosted app's container. Use when an app is wedged, or to apply changed environment variables.

tools.name: vibekit_stop_app
tools.description: Stop a hosted app's container. The app stays in your account and can be brought back with vibekit_start_app. Use to take an app offline without deleting it.

tools.name: vibekit_start_app
tools.description: Start a stopped hosted app's container. Use to bring an app stopped via vibekit_stop_app back online.

tools.name: vibekit_app_env
tools.description: Get the environment variables configured for an app (names and values). Read-only — use vibekit_set_env to change them.

tools.name: vibekit_set_env
tools.description: Set or update environment variables for an app (merges with existing). The app must be restarted or redeployed for changes to take effect.

tools.name: vibekit_delete_app
tools.description: Permanently delete a hosted app — stops the container and removes its workspace, database, env vars, and subdomain. Irreversible (the GitHub repo is NOT touched). Confirm with the user before calling.

tools.name: vibekit_chat
tools.description: Send a message to an app's built-in AI coding agent, which reads, writes, and modifies the app's code and redeploys it. Use for 'build/make a change to my app' requests. If the agent finishes quickly you get its reply directly. Otherwise you get status:'working' — do NOT resend; instead give the use

tools.name: vibekit_agent_status
tools.description: Check whether an app's AI agent is idle or working, and get its LIVE progress. Returns activity.status = the current human-readable build step (e.g. 'editing index.html', 'deploying'), activity.steps = the steps so far, and activity.done = whether the turn finished. Poll this every few seconds while

tools.name: vibekit_agent_history
tools.description: Get the recent chat history between the user and an app's AI agent.

tools.name: vibekit_enable_database
tools.description: Provision a Postgres database for an app (idempotent — safe if already enabled). Required before the vibekit_db_* tools work. Note: free-tier apps need the $3/mo Database add-on.

tools.name: vibekit_database_status
tools.description: Get an app's database status and connection info (whether it's provisioned, frozen, etc.).

tools.name: vibekit_db_schema
tools.description: Get an app's database schema — every table and its columns. Always call this before writing a query with vibekit_db_query.

tools.name: vibekit_db_query
tools.description: Run a read-only SQL query against an app's Postgres database and return up to 200 result rows. SELECT only — writes and DDL (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/ALTER/DROP/…) are rejected server-side; use vibekit_chat or vibekit_submit_task to have the agent make data or schema changes. Call vibekit_db_schema firs

tools.name: vibekit_db_table
tools.description: Browse rows of one table with pagination and optional sorting — a quick read-only alternative to hand-writing a SELECT.

tools.name: vibekit_list_deploys
tools.description: List an app's recent deploys, newest first, with status and commit info. Find a deployId here to pass to vibekit_rollback_deploy.

tools.name: vibekit_rollback_deploy
tools.description: Roll an app back to a previous deploy (get the deployId from vibekit_list_deploys). The container restarts on the rolled-back build; reversible by rolling forward again.

tools.name: vibekit_run_qa
tools.description: Kick off an automated QA run (headless-browser checks) against a hosted app. Poll vibekit_qa_status for results.

tools.name: vibekit_qa_status
tools.description: Get the latest automated QA results and status for an app.

tools.name: vibekit_submit_task
tools.description: Submit an autonomous coding task. The AI writes the code, commits to GitHub, and (by default) deploys to <subdomain>.vibekit.bot. Returns a taskId — poll vibekit_get_task or block with vibekit_wait_for_task. Use for 'build X' or 'change X' work against a repo.

tools.name: vibekit_get_task
tools.description: Get the status and result of a task submitted via vibekit_submit_task.

tools.name: vibekit_list_tasks
tools.description: List recent coding tasks, optionally filtered by status.

tools.name: vibekit_wait_for_task
tools.description: Block until a submitted task finishes (or the timeout), polling every 5 seconds, then return its result. Use right after vibekit_submit_task when you want the final result inline.

tools.name: vibekit_cancel_task
tools.description: Cancel a running task by taskId. No-op if it has already finished.

tools.name: vibekit_create_schedule
tools.description: Create a recurring coding task that the AI runs automatically on a cron schedule (e.g. a weekly SEO pass). Use for 'every Monday, do X to my repo' requests.

tools.name: vibekit_list_schedules
tools.description: List all of the account's scheduled recurring tasks.

tools.name: vibekit_delete_schedule
tools.description: Delete a scheduled recurring task by scheduleId. Confirm with the user first.

tools.name: vibekit_account
tools.description: Get VibeKit account info: plan, credit balance, and usage.

tools.name: vibekit_list_skills
tools.description: List available implementation skills (IDs, names, descriptions, tags) from the VibeKit skills registry. Discover skills here before fetching one with vibekit_get_skill.

tools.name: vibekit_get_skill
tools.description: Fetch the full content of a specific skill — implementation patterns, code examples, and best practices for a domain. Fetch on-demand when you need guidance on a specific topic.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "vibekit_restart_app" references sensitive artifacts (environment variable). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Restart a hosted app's container. Use when an app is wedged, or to apply changed environment variables.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "vibekit_app_env" references sensitive artifacts (environment variable). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get the environment variables configured for an app (names and values). Read-only — use vibekit_set_env to change them.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "vibekit_set_env" references sensitive artifacts (environment variable). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Set or update environment variables for an app (merges with existing). The app must be restarted or redeployed for changes to take effect.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "vibekit_delete_app" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Permanently delete a hosted app — stops the container and removes its workspace, database, env vars, and subdomain. Irreversible (the GitHub repo is NOT touched…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "vibekit_delete_app" references sensitive artifacts (env var). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Permanently delete a hosted app — stops the container and removes its workspace, database, env vars, and subdomain. Irreversible (the GitHub repo is NOT touched…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: vibekit_list_apps, vibekit_get_app, vibekit_chat, vibekit_agent_status, vibekit_agent_history · Private data: vibekit_get_app, vibekit_restart_app, vibekit_app_env, vibekit_set_env, vibekit_delete_app, vibekit_enable_database, vibekit_database_status, vibekit_db_schema, vibekit_db_query, vibekit_db_table · Public sink: vibekit_chat, vibekit_rollback_deploy, vibekit_submit_task · Destructive: vibekit_delete_app, vibekit_db_query, vibekit_run_qa, vibekit_delete_schedule.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: vibekit_list_apps, vibekit_get_app, vibekit_chat, vibekit_agent_status, vibekit_agent_history · Private data: vibekit_get_app, vibekit_restart_app, vibekit_app_env, vibekit_set_env, vibekit_delete_app, vibekit_enable_database, vibekit_database_status, vibekit_db_schema, vibekit_db_query, vibekit_db_table · Public sink: vibekit_chat, vibekit_rollback_deploy, vibekit_submit_task · Destructive: vibekit_delete_app, vibekit_db_query, vibekit_run_qa, vibekit_delete_schedule

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
